rationale: gpt-5.3-codex-spark is treated as a latency-optimized real-time Codex model, not as a general capability upgrade over gpt-5.4-mini.
gpt-5.4-mini is treated as a newer general small model with a higher capability floor than gpt-5.3-codex-spark.
The ranking separates capabilityRank from latencyRank so fast models can retain a latency advantage without blocking higher-capability user choices.
behaviorChanges: A user preference from router-selected gpt-5.3-codex-spark to gpt-5.4-mini is now accepted.
A user preference from router-selected gpt-5.3-codex to gpt-5.4-mini is still rejected as a genuine downgrade.
Runtime upgradeModel now upgrades gpt-5.3-codex-spark to gpt-5.4-mini before larger models.
limitations: This is a project governance ranking, not a universal benchmark claim.
No new live Codex CLI run was required; this change affects model resolution and escalation policy.
officialContext: OpenAI described GPT-5.4 mini as a newer efficient GPT-5.4-family model for coding, reasoning, tool use, and subagents.
OpenAI described GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark as a smaller Codex model optimized for ultra-low-latency real-time coding.
